Getting started with oraltags

Source: vignettes/oraltags.Rmd
oraltags.Rmd

🇪🇸 Esta página también está disponible en español: Primeros pasos.

oraltags is an R/Shiny application for the linguistic annotation and acoustic analysis of oral corpora. This page covers installation and your first session; the complete guide to the application describes every operation in detail.

Installation

Install the package straight from GitHub; the R dependencies are installed automatically:

# install.packages("remotes")   # if you don't have it yet
remotes::install_github("acabedo/oraltags", subdir = "package")

Optional dependencies:

  • praatpicture (by Rasmus Puggaard-Rode, https://github.com/rpuggaardrode/praatpicture): enables the Praatpicture subtab (multi-panel Praat-style figures).
  • irr: enables Krippendorff’s α in the Agreement tab. Without it, that column shows as N/A.
  • ffmpeg (an external binary, not an R package): if it is on the PATH, when an MP4 is loaded the sidebar viewer cuts and plays the exact clip of each intonation group. The app works fine without it.

Launching the application 

oraltags::run_app()

run_app() accepts the usual shiny::runApp() arguments (port, host, launch.browser…).

The interface is available in English and Spanish: the language selector is in the header, on the right. Switching is instant and your choice is remembered across sessions. The annotation scheme (the values stored in the analysis file) always stays in Spanish so that corpora remain comparable across teams.

Trying the bundled samples

The package ships three audio + TextGrid pairs (muestra_1, muestra_2, muestra_3). In the sidebar, section “Try a sample”, pick one and click “Load sample”: the app generates the intonation groups and you can explore every tab without your own material.

Initial screen

For the Agreement tab, three simulated analyses by three judges of muestra_1 are also included (button “Load sample analyses (judges)”).

Where your data is stored

The app never writes inside the installed package. All user data (analyses, backups, preferences and uploaded audio) is stored in a standard per-user folder:

oraltags::oraltags_data_dir()
#> e.g. "~/Library/Application Support/org.R-project.R/R/oraltags"  (macOS)
#>      "~/.local/share/R/oraltags"                                 (Linux)
#>      "C:\\Users\\<user>\\AppData\\Roaming\\R\\data\\R\\oraltags" (Windows)

Inside that folder you will find:

Subfolder Content
analisis/ One TSV per corpus (analisis_<name>.txt) and the consolidated analisis_todos.txt
backup/ Timestamped backup copies (also made before deleting an analysis)
config/ Custom variable scheme (etiquetas_variables.txt) and preferences (preferencias.txt)
audios/ Uploaded audio files, reused in future sessions

See Variables and files for the details of each format.

Typical workflow

  1. Load the audio and the transcription (or a bundled sample).
  2. Navigate with ⬅ Previous / Next ➡ or jump to a specific row.
  3. Listen to the isolated segment (▶ Segment) or with adjustable context.
  4. Acoustic metrics are computed automatically when you change row.
  5. Fill in the annotations and click Save (navigation autosaves too).
  6. Adjust the label scheme in Settings if needed.
  7. Explore the results in Statistics and export them (CSV, Excel, PNG, PDF).
  8. For reliability studies, gather the analisis_*.txt files of several judges and compute agreement in Agreement.

On this page

Developed by Adrián Cabedo-Nebot.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
